presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
1. Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log highlights gaps in documentation that must be resolved to close a control review, emphasizing verification of missing or incomplete evidence. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email governs ongoing access permissions through formal approvals, strict usage scoping, and mandatory quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation to maintain compliance.

- Access Review Evidence Log requires resolution of missing evidence to close a control; Privileged Account Approval mandates ongoing vendor confirmation and quarterly reviews to sustain access.  
- Evidence Log focuses on documentation gaps; Privileged Account Approval emphasizes access governance through scoping, logging, and recertification.  
- Control closure in the Evidence Log depends on evidence completeness; Privileged Access requires proactive renewal and oversight to remain compliant.  
- Evidence Log involves five items with mixed statuses; Privileged Access applies to two named administrators under a pilot framework.  
- Evidence Log is retrospective (verifying past gaps); Privileged Access is prospective (governing future access conditions).  
- Evidence Log is tied to a specific control review; Privileged Access is part of a broader vendor access management process.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log highlights gaps in documentation that must be resolved to close a control review, emphasizing verification of missing or incomplete evidence. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email governs ongoing access permissions through formal approvals, strict usage scoping, and mandatory quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation to maintain compliance.
Access Review Evidence Log requires resolution of missing evidence to close a control; Privileged Account Approval mandates ongoing vendor confirmation and quarterly reviews to sustain access.
Evidence Log focuses on documentation gaps; Privileged Account Approval emphasizes access governance through scoping, logging, and recertification.
Control closure in the Evidence Log depends on evidence completeness; Privileged Access requires proactive renewal and oversight to remain compliant.
Evidence Log involves five items with mixed statuses; Privileged Access applies to two named administrators under a pilot framework.
